theme: Văn hoá công sở đa quốc gia

vocab.example: We need to understand the cultural difference within our team.
vocab.example_vi: Chúng ta cần hiểu sự khác biệt văn hóa trong nhóm.

vocab.example: It takes time to adapt to a new company culture.
vocab.example_vi: Cần thời gian để thích nghi với văn hóa công ty mới.

vocab.example: Every country has a different workplace norm.
vocab.example_vi: Mỗi quốc gia có một quy chuẩn nơi làm việc khác nhau.

vocab.example: Pay attention to body language during video calls.
vocab.example_vi: Hãy chú ý đến ngôn ngữ cơ thể trong các cuộc gọi video.

vocab.meaning_vi: hòa nhập, bao gồm mọi người
vocab.example: We try to keep our meetings inclusive for everyone.
vocab.example_vi: Chúng tôi cố gắng giữ các cuộc họp mang tính hòa nhập cho mọi người.

vocab.meaning_vi: sắp xếp linh hoạt, đáp ứng
vocab.example: We accommodate different time zones when scheduling calls.
vocab.example_vi: Chúng tôi sắp xếp linh hoạt các múi giờ khi lên lịch gọi.

vocab.example: Don't make an assumption about how a colleague will react.
vocab.example_vi: Đừng vội giả định về cách đồng nghiệp sẽ phản ứng.

vocab.example: The time zone difference makes scheduling meetings tricky.
vocab.example_vi: Chênh lệch múi giờ khiến việc lên lịch họp trở nên khó khăn.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Our new project team includes people from Japan, India, and Vietnam, so we need to be mindful of the cultural difference between us.

lines.speaker: Duy
lines.text: Yes, I noticed the Japanese team is more formal, so we should adapt to their workplace norm during meetings.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Right. In our video calls, watch their body language and tone, not just their words.

lines.speaker: Duy
lines.text: Good point. We should also be inclusive and give everyone a chance to speak, even the quieter members.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: I always try to accommodate different working hours because of the time zone difference.

lines.speaker: Duy
lines.text: Same here. I schedule calls in the morning so it works for both Tokyo and Hanoi.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: One thing I've learned is not to make an assumption about how someone will react.

lines.speaker: Duy
lines.text: Exactly. It's better to ask directly than to guess.

lines.speaker: Linh
lines.text: Overall, I think our team handles the cultural difference really well.

lines.speaker: Duy
lines.text: Agreed. It just takes patience and an open mind.

questions.q: What did Linh and Duy discuss about their new project team?
options: The cultural differences in their team
The salary differences in their team
The software differences in their team

questions.q: Why does Duy schedule calls in the morning?
options: To fit both Tokyo and Hanoi time zones

questions.q: What lesson did Linh learn about assumptions?
options: Not to assume how someone will react
To assume everyone thinks the same

shadowing: We need to be mindful of the cultural difference between team members.
Let's adapt to their workplace norm during our video calls.
Watch their body language and tone, not just their words.
I try to accommodate different working hours across time zones.
It's better to ask directly than to make an assumption.
